retrieve_entity_by_id.name: retrieve_entity_by_id
retrieve_entity_by_id.description: Return a single entity by entity ID. Use after another tool has already identified the right entity and you want its properties, relations, or source sections. This is a follow-up inspection tool, not an initial search tool.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Unique entity ID.

parameters.name: contain_properties
parameters.description: Whether to include the entity's properties. Default false.

parameters.name: contain_relations
parameters.description: Whether to include the entity's relations to other entities. Default false.

parameters.name: resolve_source_documents
parameters.description: Whether to resolve source_documents into readable section or chapter titles. Default false.

retrieve_entity_by_name.name: retrieve_entity_by_name
retrieve_entity_by_name.description: Hybrid entity retrieval by name, alias, or short description, with an optional entity-type constraint.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Entity name, alias, or short description.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Optional entity type.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of candidate entities. Default 8.

parameters.name: resolve_source_documents
parameters.description: Whether to resolve source_documents into readable titles. Default false.

get_entity_sections.name: get_entity_sections
get_entity_sections.description: Return the sections or scenes where an entity appears.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Entity ID.

parameters.name: include_document_ids
parameters.description: Whether to include backing document_ids. Default true.

search_related_entities.name: search_related_entities
search_related_entities.description: Return entities connected to a given entity, optionally filtered by relation or target entity type.

parameters.name: source_id
parameters.description: Source entity ID.

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: Optional predicate filter.

parameters.name: relation_types
parameters.description: Optional relation-type whitelist.

parameters.name: entity_types
parameters.description: Optional target entity-type whitelist.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results.

parameters.name: return_relations
parameters.description: Whether to return entity-relation pairs. Default false.

get_relations_between_entities.name: get_relations_between_entities
get_relations_between_entities.description: Return graph relations of a given type between two known entities.

parameters.name: src_id
parameters.description: Source entity ID.

parameters.name: tgt_id
parameters.description: Target entity ID.

parameters.name: relation_type
parameters.description: Relation type to check.

get_common_neighbors.name: get_common_neighbors
get_common_neighbors.description: Return common graph neighbors shared by two known entities. Use for graph-structure comparison or bridge discovery when both entity IDs are known. This is exploratory and usually not a direct QA evidence tool.

parameters.name: id1
parameters.description: First entity ID.

parameters.name: id2
parameters.description: Second entity ID.

parameters.name: rel_types
parameters.description: Optional whitelist of relation types.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Edge direction constraint: any, out, or in. Default any.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of common neighbors to return.

parameters.name: include_rel_types
parameters.description: Whether to also show relation types from each side. Default false.

find_paths_between_nodes.name: find_paths_between_nodes
find_paths_between_nodes.description: Find short graph paths between two known node IDs and render them as readable evidence chains. Use only after both endpoint IDs are known and you need an abstract structural connection. This is for graph reasoning, not for retrieving exact textual evidence from the source text.

parameters.name: src_id
parameters.description: Start node ID.

parameters.name: dst_id
parameters.description: End node ID.

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Maximum path length in edges. Default 4.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of paths to return. Default 5.

top_k_by_centrality.name: top_k_by_centrality
top_k_by_centrality.description: Return the most central nodes by a stored centrality metric such as pagerank, degree, or betweenness. Use for global graph analysis or importance inspection, not for local question answering unless the question explicitly asks about central or prominent entities.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: Centrality metric: pagerank, degree, or betweenness.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Number of ranked nodes to return. Default 50.

parameters.name: node_labels
parameters.description: Optional list of node labels to restrict the ranking to.

get_co_section_entities.name: get_co_section_entities
get_co_section_entities.description: For a known entity, group co-occurring entities by section or scene. Use to inspect who or what appears together with the entity in the same local context. Helpful for narrowing candidate scenes, but not usually sufficient as final evidence on its own.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Entity ID whose co-occurring neighbors should be grouped by section.

parameters.name: include_types
parameters.description: Optional entity-type filter, for example ['Event', 'Character'].

parameters.name: document_ids
parameters.description: Optional section filter. Only return co-occurrences from these document_ids.

parameters.name: max_sections
parameters.description: Maximum number of section groups to return.

parameters.name: max_entities_per_section
parameters.description: Maximum number of co-occurring entities to show per section.

search_episodes.name: search_episodes
search_episodes.description: Search Episode nodes by hybrid keyword plus vector retrieval. Use for questions about narrative stages or mid-level plot units when Episode nodes are likely to capture the answer context. This is a structure-level locator tool, often followed by document or evidence retrieval.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Question or query describing the relevant episode content.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of episode hits to return. Default 5.

parameters.name: vector_top_k
parameters.description: How many vector candidates to keep before merging. Default 3.

parameters.name: keyword_top_k
parameters.description: How many keyword candidates to keep before merging. Default 2.

parameters.name: use_llm_filter
parameters.description: Whether to apply LLM relevance filtering to candidates.

parameters.name: llm_filter_top_k
parameters.description: How many candidates to send to the LLM filter. Default 8 or top_k, whichever is larger.

parameters.name: llm_filter_threshold
parameters.description: LLM relevance threshold. Default 0.35.

parameters.name: include_meta
parameters.description: Whether to include richer metadata such as descriptions. Default false.

search_storylines.name: search_storylines
search_storylines.description: Search Storyline nodes by hybrid keyword plus vector retrieval. Use for high-level plotline or narrative-thread questions. This is best for storyline discovery and routing, not for final fine-grained textual evidence.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Question or query describing the storyline you want to find.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of storyline hits to return. Default 5.

parameters.name: vector_top_k
parameters.description: How many vector candidates to keep before merging. Default 3.

parameters.name: keyword_top_k
parameters.description: How many keyword candidates to keep before merging. Default 2.

parameters.name: use_llm_filter
parameters.description: Whether to apply LLM relevance filtering to candidates.

parameters.name: llm_filter_top_k
parameters.description: How many candidates to send to the LLM filter. Default 8 or top_k, whichever is larger.

parameters.name: llm_filter_threshold
parameters.description: LLM relevance threshold. Default 0.35.

parameters.name: include_meta
parameters.description: Whether to include richer metadata such as descriptions. Default false.

search_sections.name: search_sections
search_sections.description: Hybrid retrieval over section-level nodes such as scenes or chapters. Useful for locating candidate sections related to the query.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Question or query.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of section hits. Default 5.

parameters.name: vector_top_k
parameters.description: Number of vector candidates before merging. Default 3.

parameters.name: keyword_top_k
parameters.description: Number of keyword candidates before merging. Default 2.

parameters.name: use_llm_filter
parameters.description: Whether to apply LLM filtering to section candidates.

parameters.name: llm_filter_top_k
parameters.description: How many candidates to send to the LLM filter. Default 8 or top_k.

parameters.name: llm_filter_threshold
parameters.description: LLM relevance threshold. Default 0.35.

parameters.name: include_related_entities
parameters.description: Whether to include related entities for each section. Default true.

parameters.name: related_entity_limit
parameters.description: Maximum related entities per type. Default 3.

parameters.name: include_meta
parameters.description: Whether to include additional metadata. Default false.

search_communities.name: search_communities
search_communities.description: Search Community nodes or summaries as high-level semantic clusters. Use for topic-level exploration or cluster routing when the question is broad. This is generally weaker than section- or sentence-level tools for direct answer evidence.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Question or topic query.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of community hits to return. Default 5.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Optional community hierarchy level to search within.

parameters.name: vector_top_k
parameters.description: How many vector candidates to keep before merging. Default 3.

parameters.name: keyword_top_k
parameters.description: How many keyword candidates to keep before merging. Default 2.

parameters.name: use_llm_filter
parameters.description: Whether to apply LLM relevance filtering to candidate communities.

parameters.name: llm_filter_top_k
parameters.description: How many candidates to send to the LLM filter. Default 8.

parameters.name: llm_filter_threshold
parameters.description: LLM relevance threshold. Default 0.35.

parameters.name: include_meta
parameters.description: Whether to include extra community properties.

parameters.name: include_member_preview
parameters.description: Whether to preview representative community members.

query_similar_entities.name: query_similar_entities
query_similar_entities.description: Vector-only semantic search over entity nodes. Use when you want entities whose meanings or descriptions are similar to a text query, especially if you do not know the exact name. This is exploratory entity recall and usually needs a follow-up evidence tool.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Semantic query text used to search similar entities.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of entity hits to return.

parameters.name: entity_types
parameters.description: Optional whitelist of entity types.

parameters.name: include_meta
parameters.description: Whether to include extra metadata fields.

retrieve_triple_facts.name: retrieve_triple_facts
retrieve_triple_facts.description: Retrieve graph triples related to a relation query.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Query describing the relation facts you want.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Maximum number of fact triples.

parameters.name: min_score
parameters.description: Optional minimum similarity score.

get_k_hop_subgraph.name: get_k_hop_subgraph
get_k_hop_subgraph.description: Return the k-hop neighborhood subgraph around one or more center nodes. Use only for explicit neighborhood inspection or structural debugging. It can expand quickly and is generally too broad for routine QA.

parameters.name: center_ids
parameters.description: List of center node IDs.

parameters.name: k
parameters.description: Neighborhood radius in hops. Keep it small, typically 1 to 3. Default 2.

parameters.name: limit_nodes
parameters.description: Maximum number of nodes to include. Default 200.
